summary.title: DFD Level 2 - Course Management
summary.description: Tightened Threat Dragon model for course management with explicit authorization, validation, and audit logging flows.

diagrams.title: Course Management
diagrams.description: Professor creates courses and sessions, students enroll in courses and view course information with authorization, validation, and audit logging.

data.name: LearningMore Backend Trust Boundary
data.description: Backend APIs, business logic, and controlled data stores for course management.

data.name: Professor
data.description: Authenticated professor user creating courses and managing sessions.

threats.title: Credential stuffing attacks on course access
threats.description: Attacker uses compromised credentials from external breaches to gain unauthorized access to course operations.
threats.mitigation: Implement account lockout after 5 failed attempts (15 min lockout). Require multi-factor authentication (MFA) for professor accounts. Monitor for credential reuse patterns.

data.name: Student
data.description: Authenticated student user enrolling in courses and viewing course information.

threats.title: Student denies course enrollment
threats.description: Student denies enrolling in course claiming unauthorized access, causing liability issues.
threats.mitigation: Capture enrollment requests with user confirmation. Log enrollment with student IP, browser fingerprint, timestamp. Require explicit student acknowledgment of course terms.

data.name: JWT + Create course (HTTPS)
data.description: Professor sends authenticated request to create course.

data.name: JWT + Enroll (HTTPS)
data.description: Student sends authenticated request to enroll in course.

data.name: JWT + View courses (HTTPS)
data.description: Student sends authenticated request to view courses.

data.name: Authorized (RBAC)
data.description: Validated professor role authorized for course creation.

data.name: Authorized (RBAC)
data.description: Validated student role authorized for enrollment.

data.name: Authorized (RBAC)
data.description: Validated professor role authorized for session management.

data.name: Authorized (RBAC)
data.description: Validated student role authorized for course viewing.

data.name: Store Course
data.description: Write course metadata and rules to Course Store.

data.name: Read course + Update
data.description: Read course data for validation, then write enrollment record.

threats.title: Role escalation - student becomes course creator
threats.description: Attacker escalates from student role to professor role to create unauthorized courses.
threats.mitigation: Validate role in JWT against course creation permission. Implement explicit role hierarchy checks. Log role usage mismatches. Monitor for unauthorized role transitions.

data.name: Read course + Store
data.description: Read course data for validation, then write session record.

data.name: Read courses
data.description: Retrieve enrolled courses for student view.

data.name: Log creation
data.description: Write course creation event to Audit Log.

data.name: Log enrollment
data.description: Write enrollment event to Audit Log.

data.name: Log session
data.description: Write session creation event to Audit Log.

data.name: Log view
data.description: Write course view event to Audit Log.

data.name: Cache poisoning
data.description: Course data retrieval and caching.

threats.title: Cache poisoning in course data retrieval
threats.description: Attacker injects malicious cached course data causing students to see incorrect course information or unauthorized courses.
threats.mitigation: Implement cache invalidation on course updates. Use cache headers (max-age: 300s). Validate cached data integrity with checksums. Implement cache bypass for sensitive queries.

data.name: Absence of repudiation
data.description: Course action repudiation risk.

threats.title: Absence of course action repudiation
threats.description: Professor denies creating a course or modifying enrollment, claiming unauthorized access due to weak audit trail.
threats.mitigation: Log all course operations with actor identification, timestamp, IP address. Include request signatures. Use digital signatures for critical operations. Retain audit logs for 7+ years.

data.name: JWT + Create sessions (HTTPS)
data.description: Professor sends authenticated request to create class sessions.

data.name: Validate Access
data.description: JWT validation, RBAC check, and enrollment verification for all course operations.

threats.title: Forged JWT bypasses course access control
threats.description: An attacker presents a forged or stolen JWT token claiming professor or student role and bypasses access validation for course operations.
threats.mitigation: Validate JWT signature using RSA public key, verify issuer, audience, expiry. Implement token revocation list (TRL) for compromised tokens. Use short-lived tokens (5-15 min) with refresh token rotation.

data.name: Create Course
data.description: Create course with metadata, schedule, and enrollment rules.

threats.title: Denial of service on course creation endpoint
threats.description: Attacker floods the course creation process with rapid requests causing service degradation.
threats.mitigation: Implement rate limiting: 10 courses per professor per hour. Use request throttling by JWT. Monitor endpoint response times. Implement circuit breaker pattern.

data.name: Manage Enrollment
data.description: Enroll student in course, validate prerequisites and capacity.

threats.title: Denial of service on enrollment endpoint
threats.description: Attacker floods course enrollment process with bulk enrollment requests exhausting system resources.
threats.mitigation: Implement rate limiting per student: 5 enrollments per minute. Implement course capacity limits. Use queue-based enrollment for high-load courses. Monitor enrollment spike patterns.

threats.title: Authorization bypass in enrollment validation
threats.description: Attacker bypasses enrollment prerequisites or capacity validation to enroll in restricted courses.
threats.mitigation: Validate prerequisites server-side before enrollment. Check course capacity dynamically. Verify enrollment rules match course definition. Implement two-phase commit for enrollment.

data.name: Manage Sessions
data.description: Create and schedule class sessions for course.

threats.title: Course session schedule tampering
threats.description: Attacker modifies class session schedules, locations, or instructors without authorization.
threats.mitigation: Restrict session modifications to course owner professor. Validate session time against course schedule. Notify enrolled students of session changes. Log modifications with change audit trail.

data.name: View Courses
data.description: Display enrolled courses and course details to student.

threats.title: Unauthorized course information disclosure
threats.description: Attacker queries course view endpoint to retrieve restricted course information (roster, contact info, internal notes).
threats.mitigation: Implement field-level security: only show enrolled students their courses and basic info. Mask sensitive fields (internal notes). Validate student enrollment before returning course details.

threats.title: Course enrollment information leakage
threats.description: Attacker infers enrollment status or student count through response time analysis or error message differences.
threats.mitigation: Normalize response times for authorization failures. Use consistent error messages. Implement request obfuscation. Log authorization failures for anomaly detection.

data.name: Course Store
data.description: Persistent course data: metadata, enrollment records, class sessions, descriptions (encrypted).

threats.title: Course data tampering - unauthorized modification
threats.description: Attacker modifies course metadata, schedule, or enrollment rules through compromised access or authorization bypass.
threats.mitigation: Implement row-level security at database layer. Validate all course modifications against course ownership. Log all updates with before/after values. Use database transactions with ACID guarantees.

threats.title: Unauthorized enrollment tampering
threats.description: Attacker modifies enrollment records to add/remove students from courses without authorization.
threats.mitigation: Enforce RBAC: only professors can modify enrollments for their courses. Validate enrollment capacity against course max enrollment. Log all enrollment changes with actor identification.

threats.title: Course Store data breach - sensitive information exposed
threats.description: Database breach exposes course metadata, enrollment records, professor contact information stored in Course Store.
threats.mitigation: Encrypt course data at rest using AES-256. Restrict database access with network segmentation. Implement database activity monitoring (DAM). Use role-based database access controls.

threats.title: SQL injection in course data queries
threats.description: Attacker injects SQL commands through course search or filter parameters to extract sensitive data.
threats.mitigation: Use parameterized queries (PreparedStatements) for all database operations. Implement input validation (whitelist course fields). Use ORM frameworks that escape queries. Implement WAF rules.

data.name: Audit Log
data.description: Immutable append-only log of course creation, enrollment changes, session scheduling, and view events (encrypted).

threats.title: Audit trail tampering - course events not logged
threats.description: Attacker modifies or deletes audit log entries to hide unauthorized course modifications.
threats.mitigation: Store audit log in immutable append-only database. Use cryptographic hashing to detect tampering. Restrict audit log access to security team only. Replicate audit logs to external system.

threats.title: Audit log compromise - deletion or modification
threats.description: Attacker gains database access and modifies audit log entries to hide course modification traces.
threats.mitigation: Implement write-once read-many (WORM) storage for audit logs. Use cryptographic signing of audit entries. Monitor audit log for deletions. Replicate to off-site secure storage.
